meta.title: aish — the AI-native shell
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.1 ships aish commit — AI-generated commit messages from your staged diff. Free software, MIT-licensed.

hero.badge: v0.1 shipped — aish commit is live

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. The first tool ships today:
turns your staged diff into a clean commit message.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

features.heading: What aish is designed to be

items.title: aish commit
items.body: The first tool ships now: stage your changes, run aish commit, and a model writes a Conventional Commits message from the diff. Confirm, --apply, or add a DCO --signoff.

items.title: Provider-agnostic
items.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, and Kilo behind one config and model alias. Never locked to a single vendor — swap models with a flag.

items.title: Open & inspectable
items.body: MIT-licensed free software, shipped as a single static Rust binary. Read the specs, audit the design, send a patch — built in the open from day one.
